content.method: Comparative case-study design selecting pilot regions that vary in governance capacity, teacher supply, and settlement type; process evaluation tracking implementation fidelity and capacity constraints alongside outcome measures; sensitivity analysis testing whether effects vary systematically with local KLIK administrative capacity.
content.question: Would a regional pilot of comprehensive de-tracking combined with zoning/choice controls (T6+T4) generalize to national implementation given Hungary's centralized (KLIK) governance constraints on local adaptation?
content.why_it_matters: T6's entire value proposition rests on producing generalizable evidence before national rollout; if centralized governance structures make pilot regions systematically unrepresentative of implementation conditions elsewhere, the pilot's evidentiary value for national policy would be substantially undermined.
